AckConfig.description: Message acknowledgement configuration for a channel.

reaction.description: Whether to add a reaction emoji when a message is received.

reaction_emoji.description: Emoji used for the "in progress" reaction.

reaction_lifecycle.description: What to do with the reaction after the agent finishes responding.

typing.description: Whether to send a typing indicator while the agent is processing.

AgentConfig.description: Per-agent configuration. Names come from the HashMap key in [`AgentsManagerConfig`].

acp_timeout_secs.description: Per-agent ACP timeout override (seconds). `None` = use manager default.

backoff.description: Per-agent backoff override. `None` = use default backoff.

crash_tracker.description: Per-agent crash tracker override. `None` = use default crash tracker.

enabled.description: Whether this agent is active. Disabled agents are not spawned.

options.description: Arbitrary key-value options passed to the agent during initialization.

tools.description: Tool names this agent is allowed to use (matched against tool config keys).

workspace.description: Execution environment (local subprocess or Docker container).

AgentsManagerConfig.description: Configuration for the agents manager and its named agent map.

acp_timeout_secs.description: Default ACP request timeout in seconds (overridable per-agent).

agents.description: Named agent configurations (keys are agent names used in routing).

shutdown_grace_ms.description: Grace period after sending shutdown before force-killing agent subprocesses (ms).

BackoffConfig.description: Per-subprocess backoff configuration for restart delays.

base_delay_ms.description: Initial delay before the first restart attempt (milliseconds).

max_delay_secs.description: Maximum delay cap — the backoff will never exceed this (seconds).

ChannelConfig.description: Per-channel configuration. Names come from the HashMap key in [`ChannelsManagerConfig`].

agent.description: Which agent to route messages to (matches an agent config key).

args.description: Extra CLI arguments passed to the channel subprocess.

backoff.description: Per-channel backoff override. `None` = use default backoff.

binary.description: Path to the channel binary (resolved via `@built-in/` at boot).

crash_tracker.description: Per-channel crash tracker override. `None` = use default crash tracker.

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active. Disabled channels are not spawned.

exit_timeout_secs.description: Per-channel graceful shutdown timeout override (seconds). `None` = use manager default.

init_timeout_secs.description: Per-channel init timeout override (seconds). `None` = use manager default.

options.description: Arbitrary key-value options passed to the channel during initialization.

ChannelsManagerConfig.description: Configuration for the channels manager and its named channel map.

channels.description: Named channel configurations (keys are channel names used in routing).

exit_timeout_secs.description: Graceful shutdown wait before force-killing channel subprocesses (seconds).

init_timeout_secs.description: Timeout for the `initialize` handshake with channel subprocesses (seconds).

CrashTrackerConfig.description:
Per-subprocess crash recovery limits.

Controls how many times an individual agent or channel subprocess may crash
within a rolling time window before the crash recovery loop gives up and
lets the manager propagate the failure upward to the Supervisor.

This is distinct from `SupervisorConfig.max_restarts` / `restart_window_secs`,
which govern manager-level restart attempts by the Supervisor itself.

max_crashes.description: Maximum number of subprocess crashes allowed within `window_secs`.

window_secs.description: Rolling window in seconds over which crashes are counted.

DockerWorkspaceConfig.description: Docker workspace: agent runs inside a Docker container.

env.description: Extra environment variables passed to the container.

network.description: Docker network to attach the container to.

pull_policy.description: Image pull policy before starting the container.

working_dir.description:
Working directory to pass as `cwd` in the ACP `session/new` handshake.
Refers to a path *inside* the agent container, not on the host.
When unset, the supervisor's own working directory is used (which is
usually wrong for Docker workspaces — set this to the container's WORKDIR).

LocalWorkspaceConfig.description: Local workspace: agent runs as a native subprocess on the host.

binary.description: Executable path (or `@built-in/` prefix resolved at boot).

env.description: Extra environment variables passed to the subprocess.

working_dir.description: Working directory for the subprocess. `None` = inherit supervisor's cwd.

LogFormat.description:
Output format for tracing/log output.

`pretty` is the default and suitable for development. `json` emits
structured JSON lines, which is preferable in production environments
where log aggregators (e.g., Datadog, CloudWatch) ingest structured output.

oneOf.description: Human-readable colored output (default, suitable for development).
Structured JSON lines (suitable for production log aggregators).

PreopenedDir.description: A host directory pre-opened for WASI filesystem access inside the WASM sandbox.

guest.description: Path visible to the WASM module.

readonly.description: Whether the directory is mounted read-only (default: `true`).

ReactionLifecycle.description:
What happens to the reaction emoji after the agent finishes responding.

- `remove`: the reaction is deleted once the response is sent
- `replace_done`: the in-progress reaction is swapped for a "done" checkmark emoji

oneOf.description: Remove the reaction emoji after the agent finishes (default).
Replace the in-progress emoji with a "done" checkmark.

SessionStoreConfig.description:
Selects the session persistence backend.

Configured under `session_store.type` in `anyclaw.yaml`.

oneOf.description: No session persistence (default). Sessions are not saved across restarts.
Persist sessions to a SQLite database.

path.description: Path to the SQLite database file. Defaults to an in-memory database when absent.

ttl_days.description: How many days of inactivity before a session is eligible for expiry cleanup.

SupervisorConfig.description:
Supervisor-level manager restart limits.

Controls how many times the Supervisor will restart a *manager* (ToolsManager,
AgentsManager, or ChannelsManager) if it exits unexpectedly, within a rolling
time window.

This is distinct from `CrashTrackerConfig`, which limits restarts of individual
agent or channel *subprocesses* within a manager's crash recovery loop.

admin_port.description: TCP port for the admin HTTP server (`/health`, `/metrics`). Default: 3000.

max_restarts.description: Maximum number of manager restart attempts within `restart_window_secs`.

permission_timeout_secs.description: Optional permission response timeout. None = block indefinitely (default).

restart_window_secs.description: Rolling window in seconds over which manager crash attempts are counted.

shutdown_timeout_secs.description: Total shutdown timeout divided equally among managers (seconds).

ToolConfig.description: Per-tool configuration. Names come from the HashMap key in [`ToolsManagerConfig`].

args.description: Extra CLI arguments passed to the tool binary.

binary.description: Path to the MCP server binary (for `Mcp` type tools).

description.description: Human-readable description of what the tool does.

enabled.description: Whether this tool is active. Disabled tools are not started.

input_schema.description: JSON Schema string describing the tool's input parameters.

module.description: Path to the `.wasm` module file (for `Wasm` type tools).

options.description: Arbitrary key-value options passed to the tool during initialization.

sandbox.description: WASM sandbox limits (fuel, timeout, memory, filesystem preopens).

tool_type.description: Whether this tool is an external MCP server or a WASM module.

ToolType.description:
Whether a tool is served by an external MCP server process or a local WASM module.

- `mcp`: spawn an external binary and communicate over JSON-RPC/stdio
- `wasm`: load a `.wasm` module and execute in the built-in sandboxed runner

oneOf.description: External MCP server subprocess communicating over JSON-RPC/stdio (default).
WASM module executed in the built-in sandboxed runner.

ToolsManagerConfig.description: Configuration for the tools manager and its named tool map.

tools.description: Named tool configurations (keys are tool names advertised to agents).

tools_server_host.description:
Host/IP for the aggregated MCP HTTP endpoint. Default `127.0.0.1`.
Set to the container hostname in Docker deployments so agent containers can reach it.

WasmSandboxConfig.description: WASM sandbox resource limits for sandboxed tool execution.

epoch_timeout_secs.description: Wall-clock timeout per invocation (seconds), enforced via epoch interruption.

fuel_limit.description: Maximum fuel (instruction count) per tool invocation.

memory_limit_bytes.description: Maximum memory the WASM module may allocate (bytes).

preopened_dirs.description: Host directories pre-opened for WASI filesystem access.

WorkspaceConfig.description: Tagged enum selecting the agent's execution environment.

oneOf.description: Run the agent as a local subprocess.
Run the agent inside a Docker container.

description:
Top-level anyclaw configuration.

Loaded from layered providers: defaults → YAML file → environment variables.
Manager-hierarchy: each manager owns its children as named maps.

agents_manager.description: Agents manager configuration and named agent map.

channels_manager.description: Channels manager configuration and named channel map.

extensions_dir.description: Base directory for resolving `@built-in/` binary paths.

supervisor.description: Supervisor-level settings (shutdown timeout, health interval, restart limits).

tools_manager.description: Tools manager configuration and named tool map.

title: AnyclawConfig
